title: Charmed Life - Chapter 18 (Fragment 3)
text:
As it took them in under its dark hood, Janet looked up into its black twistings. “The one at Glastonbury looks like this,” she said. “They say it blooms at Christmas.”

Then Cat knew they were in the heart of the garden. They were in a small bowl of meadowland. All the trees were up around the edges, except one. And here it seemed the right season of the year, because the apples were just ripening on that one tree. It stood leaning over the center of the meadow, not quite overshadowing the queer ruin there. As Janet and Cat passed quietly towards this place, they found a little spring of water near the roots of the apple tree, which bubbled up from nowhere, and bubbled away again into the earth almost at once. Janet thought the clear water looked unusually golden. It reminded her of the water from the shower when it stopped Cat from burning.

The ruins were two sides of a broken archway. There was a slab of stone which must have fallen from the top of the arch lying nearby at the foot of the tree. There was no other sign of a gate.

“I think this is it,” said Cat. He felt very sad to be leaving.

“I think it is too,” Janet agreed in an awed, muffled voice. “I feel a bit miserable to be going, as a matter of fact. How do we go?”

“I’m going to try sprinkling a pinch of dragons’ blood in the archway,” said Cat.

He fumbled out the crucible wrapped in his handkerchief from his pocket. He smelled the strong smell of the dragons’ blood and knew he was doing wrong. It was wrong to bring this harmful stuff into a place that was so strongly magic in such a different way.

But, since he did not know what else to do, Cat carefully took a pinch of the smelly brown powder between the finger and thumb of his right hand, wrapped the crucible away again with his left hand, and then, carefully and guiltily, sprinkled the powder between the pillars of broken stone.

The air between the pillars quivered like air that is hot. The piece of sunny meadow they could see beyond grew misty, then milky pale, then dark. The darkness cleared slowly, away into the corners of the space, and they found they could see into a huge room. There seemed acres of it. All of it was covered in a carpet of a rather ugly playing-card sort of design in red, blue, and yellow. The room was full of people. They reminded Cat of playing cards too, because they were dressed in stiff, bulky clothes in flat, bright colors. They were all trailing about, this way and that, looking important and agitated. The air between them and the garden was still quivering and, somehow, Cat knew they would not be able to get into the huge room.

“This is not right,” said Janet. “Where is it?”

Cat was just about to say that he did not know either, when he saw Gwendolen. She was being carried by, quite near, on a sort of bed with handholds. The eight men carrying it all wore bulky golden uniforms. The bed was gold, with gold hangings and gold cushions. Gwendolen was dressed in even bulkier clothes than the rest, that were white and gold, and her hair was done up into a high golden headdress which may have been a crown. From the way she was behaving, she was certainly a queen. She nodded to some of the important people and they leaped eagerly to the side of her bed and listened with feverish intelligence to what she was saying. She waved to some others, and they ran to do things. She made a sign at another person and he fell on his knees, begging for mercy. He was still begging when other people dragged him away. Gwendolen smiled as if this amused her. By this time, the golden bed was right beside the archway, and the space was a turmoil of people racing to do what Gwendolen wanted.

vocabulary.definition: feeling or appearing troubled or nervous
vocabulary.example: They were looking important and agitated.

vocabulary.definition: a curved structure forming an opening, typically in a wall or gate
vocabulary.example: Two sides of a broken archway.

vocabulary.definition: to flow or produce bubbles
vocabulary.example: The water bubbled up from nowhere and bubbled away again.

vocabulary.definition: larger and heavier in size or amount
vocabulary.example: Gwendolen was dressed in even bulkier clothes than the rest.

vocabulary.definition: a container used for melting or testing metals at high temperatures
vocabulary.example: He fumbled out the crucible wrapped in his handkerchief.

vocabulary.definition: showing intense enthusiasm or excitement
vocabulary.example: They listened with feverish intelligence to what she was saying.

vocabulary.definition: to handle clumsily
vocabulary.example: He fumbled out the crucible from his pocket.

vocabulary.definition: in a way that shows a feeling of guilt or remorse
vocabulary.example: He carefully and guiltily sprinkled the powder between the pillars.

vocabulary.definition: an ornamental covering worn on the head
vocabulary.example: Her hair was done up into a high golden headdress.

vocabulary.definition: an area of grassland used especially for hay
vocabulary.example: They were in a small bowl of meadowland.

vocabulary.definition: compassion or forgiveness shown towards someone
vocabulary.example: He fell on his knees, begging for mercy.

vocabulary.definition: full of mist or unclear
vocabulary.example: The piece of sunny meadow grew misty.

vocabulary.definition: to cast a shadow over; to appear more prominent or important
vocabulary.example: It stood leaning over the center of the meadow, not quite overshadowing the queer ruin there.

vocabulary.definition: to dominate or obscure the unusual remains of a building
vocabulary.example: The tree stood leaning over the center of the meadow, not quite overshadowing the queer ruin there.

vocabulary.definition: a very small amount held between fingers
vocabulary.example: He took a pinch of the smelly brown powder.

vocabulary.definition: strange or unusual
vocabulary.example: Not quite overshadowing the queer ruin there.

vocabulary.definition: to shake slightly and quickly
vocabulary.example: The air between the pillars quivered like air that is hot.

vocabulary.definition: developing to the point of maturity or readiness, especially of fruit
vocabulary.example: The apples were just ripening on that one tree.

vocabulary.definition: the remains of a destroyed building or structure
vocabulary.example: The ruins were two sides of a broken archway.

vocabulary.definition: a large, thick, flat piece of stone or other material
vocabulary.example: There was a slab of stone which must have fallen from the top of the arch.

vocabulary.definition: a natural source of water flowing from the ground
vocabulary.example: They found a little spring of water near the roots of the apple tree.

vocabulary.definition: to scatter a small amount of something over a surface
vocabulary.example: He tried sprinkling a pinch of dragons’ blood in the archway.

vocabulary.definition: a small amount of something scattered or poured over a surface
vocabulary.example: He tried sprinkling a pinch of dragons’ blood in the archway.

vocabulary.definition: a state of great disturbance or confusion
vocabulary.example: The space was a turmoil of people racing to do what Gwendolen wanted.

vocabulary.definition: complex or twisted shapes or patterns
vocabulary.example: Janet looked up into its black twistings.
